definition.notes: Algebras over a field; same OverField classifier as VectorSpaces, on Algebras(R)

entities.provenance: reverse-map vertex; MagmaticAlgebras correspondence via field base

definition.notes: R-module with bilinear multiplication; no assoc/unit required

Foundations Def 85.2: an abelian group with a left R-action and a right S-action, plus the comparison identifying the two underlying additive groups. No commutation is imposed. Both legs are underlying-additive-group functors, so this is a functor-functor pullback, not the functor-against-classifier shape used elsewhere.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 85.2; the two actions are structure and their coexistence is this pullback

Foundations Def 13.8 / 85.1. Derived, not primitive: it is the commutation locus inside BiAct(R,S) (Def 85.2, Def 85.3) -- two actions are structure, their commutation a property. Bimodules(R,R) is this family at equal parameters, not a separate node.

definition.notes: the bimodule family on the diagonal; forgetting either action to Modules(R) is neither an equivalence nor full (Foundations Prop 92.1)

entities.provenance: Foundations Prop 92.1; destination of Sage's Modules(R)

definition.notes: not VectorSpaces(K): by Prop 85.4 this is (K tensor_ZZ K)-Mod, which is K only for a prime field

entities.provenance: the bimodule diagonal over a field; destination of Sage's VectorSpaces
bedrock terminal of the preferred parent spine

definition.notes: DivisionRings := Rings.Division (nonzero multiplicative invertibility). Not Magmas.Inverse.

entities.provenance: essential image of the free R-module functor F_R (addendum Def 9.1, Cor 9.2); freeness is a property, a basis is structure (Rmk 13.7)

definition.notes: exceptional common-data host for reflection/Coxeter package
Distinct presentation from Domains; relation to ax.IntegralDomain not settled in seed
LeftModules(R) is the explicit spelling of normalized Modules(R) = R-Mod (Foundations Def 13.1). Sage's LeftModules is the one-sided category; Sage's Modules is not.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.1: R-Mod is primitive (left modules); Cor 90.2 identifies it with (R,Z)-bimodules, no choice involved. Not Sage's Modules(R), which is Bimodules(R,R).

definition.notes: Foundations Def 13.5: objects (M,s) with s : R^n -> M surjective; morphisms preserve the chosen frame. A chosen generating family, not a property.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.5; chosen finite generating presentation as structure

definition.notes: Foundations Def 13.5: objects (M,s) with s : R^n -> M an isomorphism, morphisms STRICTLY frame-preserving (fs = t). Rmk 9.3 case 2, which makes this category equivalent to Set.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.5; a chosen basis with basis-preserving morphisms

definition.notes: Foundations Def 13.6: the same objects as BasisFrame_n(R) but arbitrary R-linear maps. The morphism is the abstract linear map; its matrix relative to the two chosen bases is a derived representative (Cor 74.5). Rmk 9.3 case 1, and the convention Sage's ModulesWithBasis uses.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.6; the category for matrix calculus and change of coordinates

definition.notes: WithBasis is the basis-frame comma category over R-Mod (Def 13.5), so on a bimodule it is the pullback of BasisFrame along the left projection U_L

entities.provenance: named composite | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

definition.notes: Not an identity with left modules; Mod-R ≃ R^op-Mod under the opposite-ring substitution

entities.provenance: Sage Algebras = associative unital; MagmaticAlgebras is the Wikipedia algebra
Modules over a field; not a separate primitive

dot_import | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.
least host Magmas; named join is cat.additive_magmas
least host Sets; chosen grading (structure), not Boolean

definition.notes: Value of a construction / object-associated functor; not a category_family node
Value of a construction / object-associated functor; not a category_family node

Value of the Coslices construction at Cat_1: an object of a category C is a point * -> C. Typed as a construction_value because the seed has no entity kind for a constructed category; Lean's CategoryExpr.constructor is the right shape.

definition.notes: Object-associated construction (Picard group), not a category family
